channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Attention: First-Year Students & Graduating Seniors
item.description: Help Us Improve Your RPI Experience. Share Your Voice! Take the National Survey of Student Engagement (NSSE) survey to help RPI improve your educational experience. Check your email for your personal invitation to participate!
item.author: Students

item.title: Spring 2026 Student Course Evaluations Now Open
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 (all day) - Spring 2026 course evaluations are now open. The evaluation period will be open from April 14th through April 30th. Your feedback is very important to the Institute, the five schools, and their departments, so please complete evaluation surveys for all your courses! Evaluations will be available through Thursday, April 30, 2026.
item.author: Students

item.title: Graduating Students join us for RPI’s Lavender Graduation on May 13!
item.description: Join us in celebrating Rensselaer students of the LGBTQ+ community at Lavender Graduation! This is sure to be an inclusive and celebratory experience. We look forward to acknowledging your accomplishments and evolutions into college graduates of Rensselaer. Business casual recommended but come as you feel comfortable. Graduating students who wish to be recognized must register at the linked attached by Sunday, May 10. For Questions, email the LGBTQ+ Task Force at lgbtqtaskforce@rpi.edu.
item.author: Students

item.title: Graduating Students join us for the Intercultural Celebration on May 12!
item.description: Join us at the Intercultural Celebration, as part of RPI’s Graduating Student Week, this casual events recognizes and honors the achievements, experiences, and contributions of graduating students from a wide range of identities and backgrounds within the RPI community. Graduating students are invited to join this celebration to reflect on their journeys, connect with peers with Graduation Cap Decorating, Hors d’oeuvres, music, games, and Photo Ops with our RPI Banner! Register today!
item.author: Students

item.title: Last Day of Drop-In Tutoring for Spring 2026 Semester is Wednesday, 4/29
item.description: The last day of our regular drop-in tutoring will be Wednesday, 4/29 for the Spring 2026 semester at the Success Center, Nason, and ECAV locations. Small group, CSTEP, and One-to-One Tutoring is still available to book until Wednesday, 5/6. Goodluck with finals week!
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Review Sessions Week of 4/26-5/3
item.description: -Diff Eq: Sunday, 4/26 (3-5pm) at the Success Center -Math Methods: Monday, 4/27 (7-9pm) at ECAV: Harkness Room (Room 3211) -Data Management: Monday, 4/27 (7-9pm) at ECAV: Championship Room (Room 3130) -Data Structures: Monday, 4/27 (8-10pm) at the Success Center -CAL II: Monday, 4/27 (7-9pm) in the Nason Classroom
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI Department of Economics Spring Seminar Series: Kyunghoon Ban, Rochester Institute of Technology
item.description: Please join us as the Department of Economics welcomes Dr. Kyunghoon Ban, who will present: “Difference-in-Differences Models in the Presence of Mediators” next Wednesday, April 29th at 3:30 PM in SAGE 5510.
item.author: Community

item.title: Joint Cognitive Science and Computer Science Colloquium with Dr. Ora Lassila
item.description: Please join the Cognitive Science and Computer Science Departments on Wednesday, April 29th, at 12pm in Carnegie 113 for a seminar by Dr. Ora Lassila titled “Symbolic AI in the Age of LLMs”. Pizza and refreshments will be provided.
item.author: Community

item.title: Summer 2026 Meal Plan Announcement
item.description: For Summer 2026, only Block Plans will be offered. These plans are available to students living on or off campus during the summer semester. Students who will be back living on campus for Fall 2026 are required to enroll in a residential meal plan for that semester. Additionally, Student and Community RAD may be used at all campus dining and retail locations. For more information please click the link above or email auxiliaryservices@rpi.edu with any questions.
item.author: Community

item.title: Starman
item.description: Wednesday / April 29, 2026 at 4:30 pm From Academy Award®-nominated director Robert Stone, a New York Times Critics Pick documentary. In this meditative, intergalactic biopic, we follow Gentry Lee, Chief Engineer for Planetary Exploration at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory and sci-fi writer, on his journey through space and on Earth. From the Viking and Voyager missions to co-authoring the future with Arthur C. Clarke, Lee’s life has been spent with his head in the stars and his feet on the…
item.author: Community

item.title: Systems Engineering & Technology Management Review on April 29
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 29, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - Join RPI at Work on this 30-minute information session hosted by Dean Aric Krause, Ph.D., and will focus on the Systems Engineering and Technology Management graduate degree. The degree is digitally delivered, flexible, and project-based to prepare decision-makers with broader and deeper capabilities in leading dynamic organizations. The session will highlight program details and how you can select graduate certificates relevant to your work…
item.author: Community

item.title: ECSE Seminar: “Understanding and Using Embedding Spaces of Large Models”
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 29, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Join us for an ECSE Seminar: “Understanding and Using Embedding Spaces of Large Models” with Prof. Anand Sarwate of Rutgers. Abstract and speaker bio can be found at the event URL.
item.author: Community

item.title: TVCOG RPI Night | April 28
item.description: Join the RPI community for an evening at the Tech Valley Center of Gravity, sponsored by the RPI Lally School of Management, from 6–8 PM. Explore a 14,000 sq. ft. makerspace, connect with local alumni, and enjoy free pizza. Open to all students and located near the Blitman shuttle stop. RSVP via the link above.
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Chemistry Seminar Series
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 28, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Catherine Tremblay, Mass Spectrometry Sales Specialist at Waters Corporation, will speak on the topic “People, Mass Spec, and the Power of Staying in Touch”.
item.author: Community

item.title: Math Colloquium: Dan Fortunato (Flatiron Institute)
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 29, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - This Mathematical Sciences Colloquium will feature a presentation titled “Accelerating high-order methods for PDEs using fast direct solvers” by Dr. Dan Fortunato. He is an Associate Research Scientist in the Center for Computational Mathematics and the Center for Computational Biology at the Flatiron Institute. His research focuses on spectral methods, fast methods for PDEs, integral equations, computational fluid & solid mechanics, and…
item.author: Community

item.title: Six RPI students awarded National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowships
item.description: Six RPI students have been awarded National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowships. A highly prestigious and competitive award, the fellowship supports full-time graduate students with the potential to make significant contributions in STEM. Congratulations to undergraduate students Chloe Sharp, Nadine Grant, and Jayashree Balaraman; graduate students Jaron Shoemaker and Katie Sullivan; and alum Nilaish Sen; plus recipients of honorable mentions Ethan Fisk and alum Phoebe Demers.
item.author: Community

item.title: AVS Hudson Mohawk Spring Meeting
item.description:
Date: Wednesday, April 29, 2026 1:30 PM - 7:30 PM - The AVS Hudson Mohawk Spring Meeting will be held on April 29th at RPI. The meeting features a keynote by Dr. Christine M. Mahoney (Corning R&D), researcher talks, and a student poster session. Topics include materials, interfaces, nanofabrication, energy, and bio-inspired systems. Abstracts are due April 15; registration by April 22.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
